questions.question: When did the storming of the Bastille take place?
questions.answer: The storming of the Bastille took place on the morning of 14 July 1789 in Paris.

questions.question: Who was the King of France during the revolution?
questions.answer: Louis XVI of the Bourbon family was the King of France when the revolution broke out.

questions.question: Name the three estates into which French society was divided.
questions.answer: French society was divided into the First Estate (Clergy), Second Estate (Nobility), and Third Estate (Big businessmen, merchants, peasants, etc.).

questions.question: What was the 'Tithe'?
questions.answer: The Tithe was a tax levied by the Church, comprising one-tenth of the agricultural produce.

questions.question: What was the 'Taille'?
questions.answer: The Taille was a direct tax to be paid by the members of the Third Estate to the State.

questions.question: Define 'Subsistence Crisis'.
questions.answer: A subsistence crisis is an extreme situation where the basic means of livelihood are endangered.

questions.question: Who wrote the book 'Two Treatises of Government'?
questions.answer: John Locke wrote 'Two Treatises of Government', in which he sought to refute the doctrine of the divine and absolute right of the monarch.

questions.question: Name the philosopher who proposed the division of power within government.
questions.answer: Montesquieu proposed a division of power between the legislative, the executive, and the judiciary in his book 'The Spirit of the Laws'.

questions.question: What was the Estates General?
questions.answer: The Estates General was a political body to which the three estates sent their representatives to pass proposals for new taxes.

questions.question: Where did the representatives of the Third Estate take the Tennis Court Oath?
questions.answer: They took the oath in the hall of an indoor tennis court on the grounds of Versailles on 20 June 1789.

questions.question: Who led the National Assembly representatives at the Tennis Court?
questions.answer: The representatives were led by Mirabeau and Abbé Sieyès.

questions.question: What was the main object of the draft Constitution of 1791?
questions.answer: The main object was to limit the powers of the monarch and distribute them among different institutions like the legislature, executive, and judiciary.

questions.question: Who were 'Active Citizens' in France in 1791?
questions.answer: Active Citizens were men above 25 years of age who paid taxes equal to at least 3 days of a labourer's wage and had the right to vote.

questions.question: Name the national anthem of France composed during the revolution.
questions.answer: The 'Marseillaise', composed by the poet Roget de L’Isle, became the national anthem of France.

questions.question: Which political club was the most successful during the revolution?
questions.answer: The Jacobin Club was the most successful political club, led by Maximilian Robespierre.

questions.question: What does 'Sans-culottes' mean?
questions.answer: It literally means 'those without knee breeches,' referring to the long striped trousers worn by Jacobin members to distinguish themselves from nobles.

questions.question: When was France declared a Republic?
questions.answer: France was declared a Republic on 21 September 1792 after the monarchy was abolished.

questions.question: Which period is referred to as the 'Reign of Terror'?
questions.answer: The period from 1793 to 1794 is known as the Reign of Terror, during which Robespierre followed a policy of severe control and punishment.

questions.question: What was the 'Guillotine'?
questions.answer: The guillotine was a device consisting of two poles and a blade with which a person was beheaded, named after Dr. Guillotin.

questions.question: What was the 'Directory'?
questions.answer: The Directory was an executive body of five members appointed to run the government after the fall of Robespierre to avoid concentration of power.

questions.question: Who was Olympe de Gouges?
questions.answer: She was one of the most important politically active women in revolutionary France who protested against the exclusion of women from rights.

questions.question: When did women in France finally win the right to vote?
questions.answer: Women in France won the right to vote in 1946.

questions.question: When was slavery finally abolished in French colonies?
questions.answer: Slavery was finally abolished in French colonies in 1848.

questions.question: Who crowned himself Emperor of France in 1804?
questions.answer: Napoleon Bonaparte crowned himself Emperor of France in 1804.

questions.question: Name two Indian individuals who responded to the ideas of the French Revolution.
questions.answer: Tipu Sultan and Rammohan Roy are two Indian individuals who responded to the ideas coming from revolutionary France.
